title: Level 383
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 383: "Only Way is Up"}} {{CSS|User:Fungrly Scrunkly/ROCKS.css}}
{{Class 3e}} Level 383, colloquially known as 'The Only Way is Up,' is the 384th level of the Backrooms. == Description == {{Blurimage|file=RockClimb.jpg|caption=A photograph of a lone wanderer traversing Level 383.}} Upon no-clipping into Level 383, the first thing one will notice is that they are hanging on for dear life on a mountain. If the wanderer looks down, they will notice a dense, green fog that will slowly envelop the level as the wanderer traverses through it. This fog is incredibly neurotoxic, and will result in the death of anyone that inhales it in under a minute. If one does not immediately start going up, the fog will envelop them; leading to their death. The only equipment that a wanderer will have upon entering the level is a tether and some anchors, useful for anyone that requires a break whilst climbing. Though do know that that break should not be too long, as the looming threat of the fog is still present. One does not start with a helmet or safety equipment, further increasing the danger of Level 383. Once a wanderer has reached the mid-way mark of the mountain, a large ledge will be spotted. In the middle of this ledge is a medium-sized, red flag. Surrounding the flag are various objects that may be useful for the rest of the wanderer's ascent; these include, but are not limited to, [[Object 1|Almond Water]], helmets, a pouch, and various energy bars that seem to be rip-offs of popular brands from [[The Frontrooms]]. Whilst one is atop the ledge, the fog will remain motionless though right below the wanderers feet. Following a wanderer's mid-way mark across the mountain, rocks will occasionally descend from the sky; not the mountain. These rocks are simply summoned above the wanderer, typically around 5km above. In order to get away from these rocks, one must very quickly scurry to the left or right before it falls on them. When these rocks hit someone, they will shatter and cause great pain - and sometimes a concussion - in the person it hit. If rocks fall upon a person multiple times, death is a likely occurrence. === Damp Caverns === ----
"As I climb across the mountain's surface, I notice an obstruction in my path. It brought me to a cavernous tunnel that lead to the other side of the mountain. The light was almost blinding."
~ Someone who had discovered a damp cavern.
{{Blurimage|file=Realistic-3d-render-cave-600nw-1985984312.webp|caption=A photograph taken within a damp cavern.|align=left}} Most commonly found by wanderers following the mid-way mark, '''damp caverns''' are obstructions in a wanderer's path that lead to the other side of the mountain. One must traverse through these tunnels with haste, as the fog slowly encroaches upon them. The end of the tunnel is easily noticeable, as the light from the outdoors is incredibly bright compared to the dark, damp cave. The fluid found circulating within these cavernous tunnels has been noted to be - or at least have a chemical structure extremely similar to - [ DATA LOST ]. Meaning ingestion of this fluid is incredibly dangerous and can lead to many dangerous issues in the long run, and therefore is not advised to be consumed. Some have noted that entities lie within these tunnels, describing them as creatures that simply watch them as they quickly traverse through the tunnel. These entities have yet to be properly researched, and their existence is rather speculative, too; with information on them only being based on hearsay and rumors. It has recently been reported that the phenomena where rocks spontaneously fall from the sky can be combined with damp caverns. It is much more dramatic in damp caverns, however, as it can result in someone getting caved in the tunnel. The combination can be characterized by a faint sound of crumbling before the rocks fall. If this sound is noticed, a wanderer can easily run through the cave and get to the other side, though if the sound is left unheard, then the results can be much more catastrophic. ===The Fog=== ---- {{Blurimage|file=GREEN.jpg|caption=A photograph of the Fog encompassing the ground of the level.}} The Fog is a highly neutrotoxic, gaseous substance that slowly encroaches on wanderers who do not quickly ascend up the mountain. Upon inhalation of the Fog, it attacks ones nervous system, causing extreme pain and eventual death of the wanderer who have inhaled it. Death to the Fog is a gradual and painful process, and being engulfed in the Fog is certain death. Some have noted that regular consumption of Almond Water across the trip can slow down the process of death after inhaling the Fog, though there have been no trustable reports of Almond Water completely halting the process. Those with autoimmune problems have been proven to be more susceptible to the Fog, and fall to its effects much faster. ==Ingress & Egress== ===Ingress=== *One may enter Level 383 via traversing upward a frail staircase leading to an astonishingly bright hole in [[Level 8]]; *In open levels, one has a chance to find a mountain with colorful, in-door rock climbing 'rocks.' Traversal up this mountain will lead one to be transported to Level 383. ===Egress=== *If one reaches the top of the mountain, they will be sent to a random level with a class from zero to two. *It is believed that, if one noclips through the fallen rocks whilst caved in a damp cavern, one will end up in [[Level 8]].
